title: allohemia pedunculata
intro: Allohemia pedunculata, also known as Indian walnut or elephant apple, is a tropical tree native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. Allohemia pedunculata has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. Herbal preparations of Allohemia pedunculata include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and supplements for medicinal purposes.
